name: Scotch Salmon
recipeInstructions:
1. Dust a 6-7 ounce salmon filet lightly with flour.
2. In a pan, heat 2 tablespoons of clarified butter over medium heat.
3. Add the salmon fillet to the pan and brown on one side for about 2-3 minutes.
4. Season the fillet with salt and white pepper to taste.
5. Flip the fillet and add minced garlic, shallot, Dijon mustard, and brown sugar.
6. Cook for a few seconds until fragrant.
7. Add raspberry vinegar and return the fillet to the pan.
8. Deglaze the pan with a splash of Scotch (1/4 cup) and orange juice (1/4 cup).
9. Stir the mixture in a circular motion until well combined.
10. Reduce heat to low and simmer until the sauce begins to thicken.
11. Finish by stirring in heavy cream (1/4 cup) and Drambuie (3 tablespoons).
12. Serve garnished with crème fraîche and candied orange zest.
